title: (Problems with date calcs) This is all kinds of crazy
excerpt: This has me stumped. I’m having date calculation issues on a board, so I made a simpler version for testing. Can you explain this behavior?

posts.author: jscottsmith (Scott Smith)
posts.cookedHtml:
This has me stumped. I’m having date calculation issues on a board, so I made a simpler version for testing. Can you explain this behavior?

image1109×738 67.8 KB

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@jscottsmith

Just kidding… well, kind-of…

The comparisons are evaluating the dates as strings. “T”>“J”=TRUE, F>J= FALSE, etc.

This will give you the expected results:

FORMAT_DATE({X Days Later}, "Y-MM-DD") > FORMAT_DATE({Janurary 1}, "Y-MM-DD")

Jim - The Monday Man (YouTube Channel)
Watch Our Latest Video: Column Total in monday.com Formulas? YES!!!

posts.author: jscottsmith (Scott Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: Ah… ok. It’s strange because the code I have in place worked the last few months. I wondered if something changed. I’ll give that a try. Thanks!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@jscottsmith

I’m very curious as to why it might have worked then stopped. Let me know how it turns out.

Jim
